# Transcrição Completa: Using Sales Videos to Aid The Sales Process

 

[00:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

**TEXTO:** "6 Using Sales Videos to Aid The Sales Process"

**NÚMEROS:** None

**VISUAL:** Título do vídeo com ícones de vídeos sendo usados como ferramentas de vendas

 

[00:02] All these videos that have been on landing pages like mini webinars, full-time webinars, I mean really any other sales video that you've used to generate applications and scheduled calls into the business. Remember what I said? There's a huge statistic to keep in mind.

 

[00:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

**TEXTO:** "Video Engagement Statistics"

**NÚMEROS:** 2,000 people hit page, <5% watched, max 9% pressed play

**VISUAL:** Gráfico mostrando baixíssima taxa de engajamento com vídeos

 

[00:25] 2,000 people a day hit this client's page, less than 5% of people watched it. I looked at all the other clients that were running this same type of funnel and it was very similar statistics. At most, it was like 9% of people hit the page actually watched the video.

 

[00:45] And by watch the video, I literally mean just pressing play. I don't actually mean watching it through all the way. I just mean 5 to 9% of people press play.

 

[01:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

**TEXTO:** "Videos Aren't Dead - Change How We Use Them"

**NÚMEROS:** None

**VISUAL:** Evolução do uso de vídeos - de entry point para sales aid

 

[01:05] Now, does that mean these videos are dead? No. It just means we have to change how we use those videos. So, as the business owner, as a salesperson in the business, you have to use these things as a sales aid.

 

[01:20] Now, they're no longer the entry point into the funnel. They're at a lower point in the process of buying.

 

[01:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

**TEXTO:** "New Consumer Behavior"

**NÚMEROS:** None

**VISUAL:** Fluxo: Form fill → Contact → Questions → Video as aid

 

[01:35] So, as an example, if the entry point in the past has been videos, it's no longer videos. Now, consumer behavior shows people are very willing to fill out forms where they express interest, answer a few questions and then they expect to be contacted.

 

[01:55] Once they are contacted via phone or text, you've successfully talked to this person. This is when the sales video comes into play.

 

[02:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

**TEXTO:** "Example Text Strategy"

**NÚMEROS:** 5 minutes now, later today, or text questions

**VISUAL:** Exemplo de mensagem com três opções

 

[02:15] We can ask questions to people like, "Hey, I saw that you filled out a form online. I have five minutes to talk now or I can talk later today or you can just shoot me over a few questions via text."

 

[02:30] Let's say that they just choose the option to text you and they go back and forth with maybe one to two questions. My bet is your sales video probably answers a few of those questions that they're going to have in the initial contact phase.

 

[02:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

**TEXTO:** "Answer + Video Strategy"

**NÚMEROS:** None

**VISUAL:** Combinação de resposta direta + vídeo complementar

 

[02:55] When they text you over a few questions, make sure you answer them. Make sure that even if you have a full video that you still give them an answer, but make sure you also plug the video as an option to get a lengthier answer.

 

[03:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

**TEXTO:** "Example: $40K Amazon Automation"

**NÚMEROS:** $40,000 investment, 18 months guarantee

**VISUAL:** Produto de automação Amazon, garantia de recompra

 

[03:20] So, as an example, let's use a $40,000 product for an Amazon automation offer. I'm selling an offer where people are going to invest 40K. They'll get paid a monthly amount based on the sales that their Amazon store produces. We guarantee that revenue comes in or we'll buy the store back after 18 months.

 

[03:45] We've got a customer that just filled out a form about that offer. They said yes, they have 40K liquid and they said yes, they understand how an Amazon automation business works.

 

[04:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

**TEXTO:** "Real Conversation Example"

**NÚMEROS:** None

**VISUAL:** Conversa via texto sobre garantia

 

[04:05] Now, we've reached out. "Hey, what's going on, lead? Saw you filled out a form about our Amazon automation business. I have five minutes to talk now if you're available or I can do later today or you can just shoot me over a text with any questions you have."

 

[04:20] Customer says, "Yeah, I actually just had a few quick questions. How's the guarantee work?"

 

[04:30] I could answer the question by saying, "Hey, here's how a guarantee works. Here's some details about it. And by the way, here's a short video that goes over how that guarantee works in a little more detail if you want to watch it."

 

[04:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

**TEXTO:** "Create Videos for Common Questions"

**NÚMEROS:** 1-3 minute videos

**VISUAL:** Lista de perguntas frequentes sendo transformadas em vídeos curtos

 

[04:50] That is a great way to both answer the question and get the person to potentially watch a video. Now, to be clear, we want to consider that the most commonly asked questions that we get should be turned into videos with lengthier answers.

 

[05:10] So, as an example, if you analyzed all of what you are asked in these text messages or on these phone calls with potential leads, you want to in turn give that feedback to people like me who are the marketer.

 

[05:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

**TEXTO:** "Feedback Loop: Sales → Marketing"

**NÚMEROS:** None

**VISUAL:** Fluxo de feedback de vendas para marketing para criação de vídeos

 

[05:30] So, I can go tell the client what type of videos to shoot. Or you need to tell the business owner directly, hey, I'm getting this question a lot. This would be great if we turned it into a video.

 

[05:45] And then you want to have little, you know, one to three minute videos. Then answer those common questions.

 

[05:55] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

**TEXTO:** "Second Question Example"

**NÚMEROS:** 3-5 months to profit, $200-$2000 first month

**VISUAL:** Timeline de lucro e valores esperados

 

[06:00] "Here's a short video on how the guarantee works." "Oh, great. That was really helpful. Thank you. I had another question. How long does it typically take for the store to start making profit?"

 

[06:15] "Great question, lead. Typically takes anywhere from three to five months for the first sales to start rolling in for you to really start making some profit. The average amount a customer makes is anywhere from a couple hundred bucks in the first month. Sometimes they make a couple thousand dollars."

 

[06:35] "Here's a short video going over that in more details on how the launch process works with our business and when you can expect to start making profit with your Amazon store."

 

[06:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

**TEXTO:** "Don't Force Videos - Give Answers First"

**NÚMEROS:** 10 seconds to read vs 1 minute to watch

**VISUAL:** Comparação de tempo: texto vs vídeo

 

[06:55] See, videos are extremely helpful, but we don't want to force people to watch them. We still want to give people the answers. I'm the type of buyer where those little answers that you give me, that'll be more than enough.

 

[07:15] If I'm not clear on it, then I'll watch the video and I'll appreciate that you put it there. I'm not going to expect though that you'd respond by only sending a video.

 

[07:30] So, let's use the guarantee as an example again. "Hey, I just had a quick question. How does the guarantee work?" "Yeah, here's a one minute video going over that." Dude, it takes me 10 seconds to read your text. It takes me one full minute to watch a video.

 

[07:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

**TEXTO:** "Consumer Behavior Reality"

**NÚMEROS:** 10 seconds read vs 1 minute video

**VISUAL:** Preferências de consumo de conteúdo

 

[07:55] I'm going to bias towards that short answer 10 times out of 10. Sure, different consumers are different. Typically, highly detailed people will be one of the only people that'll actually go through every video that you send them.

 

[08:15] Whereas most people, once again, they're going to be very conservative with giving your business a lot of time, especially when they're just going through the interested phase and trying to figure out whether it's for them or not.

 

[08:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

**TEXTO:** "Sales Videos as Tools"

**NÚMEROS:** None

**VISUAL:** Vídeos sendo usados estrategicamente no processo de vendas

 

[08:40] So remember sales videos as a tool. I want to have my main presentation videos now used farther down in the sales process. I also want to create several smaller videos that can be used as what we call sales aids.

 

[08:55] These videos simply put are going to be individually sent out. They're typically the most commonly asked questions and we're going to use them as well in our sales process after we've established contact and people start asking questions.

 

[09:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

**TEXTO:** "More Data = Higher Probability"

**NÚMEROS:** None

**VISUAL:** Gráfico mostrando relação entre consumo de conteúdo e conversão

 

[09:20] Obviously, the more data we can get somebody to consume, simply put, the higher the probability is that they're going to buy from us or at least be willing to jump on a call.

 

[09:35] Make sure you develop all these videos. Don't be lazy about it. They help a ton with making more money and getting people on the phones to close.

 

**🔥 ANÁLISE FURION POWER: Jeremy REVOLUCIONA completamente o uso de vídeos de vendas! A estatística brutal de 5-9% de engajamento DESTRÓI a ilusão de que vídeos funcionam como entry point. Mas Jeremy não abandona os vídeos - ele os transforma em ARMAS TÁTICAS precisas! A estratégia de responder primeiro + vídeo complementar é GENIAL - respeita o tempo do prospect enquanto oferece profundidade. Jeremy entende que consumidores modernos querem eficiência, não palestras forçadas!**
